description: Use Striatum daemon MCP mutation tools safely, including workflow generation preview/write, capability scopes, denial recovery, and audit expectations.

# Striatum MCP Mutation Surface

## When to invoke

- You have an operator-issued capability token and want to use daemon MCP instead of CLI commands.
- You want to preview or write a generated workflow through the local API/chat path.
- You need to recover from `capability_missing`, `token_revoked`, `token_expired`, `method_unknown`, or `mutations_disabled`.

## Authoritative reference

Call `tools/list` first. It returns the effective tool set for the current token, after intersecting method registry entries with token capabilities and repository scope. `tools/call` re-authorizes every call, so visibility is not the security boundary.

Use `daemon.hello` / `daemon.welcome` for version handshake and `daemon.describe` for the broader operator-side method registry. `daemon.describe` can reveal version skew, but it does not prove the current token may call a method.

Mutating calls append metadata-only audit rows, including denials. Audit rows carry hashes and method metadata, not transcripts or raw generated workflow content.

## Common patterns

Effective tool set:

```json
{"method":"tools/list","params":{"token":"<capability-token>"}}
```

Preview before write:

```json
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"workflow.generate.preview","arguments":{"spec":{"schema_version":"striatum.workflow_generator.v1","shape":"code_change","lane_set":"author_reviewer","workflow_id":"my-change","name":"My change","workflow_version":"2026-05-12","branch":{"mode":"confirm","suggested_name":"striatum/my-change","allow_dirty":false},"scaffold_root":"workflows/my-change","artifact_root":"striatum/my-change","lanes":{"author":{"display_model":"Codex GPT-5.5","command":["codex","exec"]},"reviewer":{"display_model":"Claude Opus","command":["claude","--print"]}},"options":{"max_revision_cycles":1}}}}}
```

Write after preview and operator approval:

```json
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"workflow.generate","arguments":{"spec":{"schema_version":"striatum.workflow_generator.v1","shape":"code_change","lane_set":"author_reviewer","workflow_id":"my-change","name":"My change","workflow_version":"2026-05-12","branch":{"mode":"confirm","suggested_name":"striatum/my-change","allow_dirty":false},"scaffold_root":"workflows/my-change","artifact_root":"striatum/my-change","lanes":{"author":{"display_model":"Codex GPT-5.5","command":["codex","exec"]},"reviewer":{"display_model":"Claude Opus","command":["claude","--print"]}},"options":{"max_revision_cycles":1}},"confirm_write":true}}}
```

`confirm_write: true` is still not enough in web chat. The chat UI must also record a separate operator confirmation gesture before Striatum writes files.

## Capability scope

A repo-scoped write token for repo A cannot write repo B. Ask the operator for the narrow token that matches the target repository instead of retrying with the wrong scope.

`tools/list` filtering is not a security boundary. It is an ergonomic projection. `tools/call` is the real authorization point and fails closed for missing, expired, revoked, or wrong-scope capabilities.

## Denial recovery

| Denial | Agent response |
|---|---|
| `capability_missing` | Stop the attempted mutation and ask the operator for a short-lived token with the named capability and correct repository scope. |
| `token_revoked` | Stop retrying; treat revocation as an operator decision. |
| `token_expired` | Ask for a fresh token with the same narrow scope. |
| `method_unknown` | Re-read `tools/list`; if still absent, ask the operator to inspect `daemon.describe` for version skew. |
| `mutations_disabled` | Do not retry. The service was intentionally started without mutation authority; the operator must restart it with `--allow-mutations` if they want writes. |

## What not to do

- Do not claim a different identity to bypass capability gates.
- Do not write directly to `.striatum/state.sqlite3`.
- Do not request wildcard or admin capabilities for ordinary workflow generation.
- Do not loop on `token_revoked`.
- Do not treat audit as malicious-local-root-resistant proof.
